title: Meta Ads - Health and wellness claims
text: Meta advertising policies restrict certain health and wellness claims. Advertisers must not make exaggerated or absolute claims about health outcomes (e.g. "cure", "guaranteed results"). Supplements and health products require clear disclaimers. Claims must be supported by evidence; before/after imagery has specific rules. Restricted categories include weight loss guarantees, misleading before/after, and unsubstantiated health claims. See Meta Business Help for the full policy.

title: CPC benchmarks by industry
text: CPC (Cost Per Click) benchmarks vary by industry and region. Typical ranges: Legal often $5–$15, Finance $2–$8, E-commerce $0.5–$2, B2B $3–$10. CPM (Cost Per Mille) for display can be $1–$5 for broad targeting. These are indicative; actual costs depend on competition, quality score, and targeting. New marketplace sellers should start with small test budgets and scale based on CPA (Cost Per Action) and ROAS.

title: TikTok ad formats overview
text: TikTok offers In-Feed Ads (native video), TopView (first thing users see), Brand Takeover, and Spark Ads (boosting organic posts). Video length is typically 9–15 seconds; vertical format required. Best for awareness and engagement; conversion tracking available. Budget pacing can be daily or lifetime. New advertisers are encouraged to use Spark Ads or In-Feed with clear CTAs.
